subject: Sociology
title: Human Rights and Global Justice
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Human Rights and Global Justice.

hook.text: The Universal Declaration of Human Rights promised dignity for all in 1948. Millions still wait.

buildup.text: Human rights frameworks assert that all people deserve basic protections regardless of nationality or culture.

discovery.text: International courts, treaties, and NGOs enforce human rights, but enforcement remains weak and selective.

twist.text: Powerful nations violate rights with impunity. Weak nations face sanctions. Justice follows the global power map.

climax.text: Global justice is an aspiration, not a reality. Its promise matters even when its practice falls short.

punchline.text: Rights exist on paper; power decides in practice.

quiz.question: What limits global human rights enforcement?
options: Powerful nations can violate rights with less consequence
All nations are held equally accountable
